name: agent-audit
description: Static security scanner for LLM agents — prompt injection, MCP config auditing, taint analysis. 49 rules mapped to OWASP Agentic Top 10 (2026). Works with LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen.

commit.message:
chore: exclude test fixtures from GitHub secret scanning

Benchmark datasets (AVB), test fixtures, and demo files contain
intentional fake tokens for security scanner evaluation. Exclude
these paths to prevent false-positive secret scanning alerts.
fix: add skip-existing to PyPI publish to avoid duplicate upload errors
chore: bump version to 0.18.2
fix: resolve mypy type errors in skill scanner integration
fix: remove unused imports flagged by ruff (F401)
fix: use dynamic version in e2e tests instead of hardcoded 0.17.0

fix: v0.18.1 — fix rule loading path and sync all YAML rule files

The scan.py rule directory lookup used Path(__file__).parent.parent
(2 parents: commands->cli) instead of .parent.parent.parent
(3 parents: commands->cli->agent_audit), so bundled rules in
agent_audit/rules/builtin/ were never found by PyPI installs.

Also syncs all 5 YAML rule files from monorepo rules/builtin/ into
the package, adding langchain_security_v030.yaml and
mcp_security_v030.yaml which were previously missing.

Additional changes in this release:
- SKILL.md security scanners (AGENT-058~064)
- Updated asi_coverage_v030.yaml with AGENT-052, AGENT-053
- Engine and scanner enhancements
docs: add OpenClaw skill installation instructions
fix: remove unused variable flagged by ruff (F841)

feat: v0.17.0 MCP security enhancement — 4 new rules (AGENT-054~057)

Add MCP rug pull / baseline drift detection (AGENT-054), cross-server
tool shadowing (AGENT-055), tool description poisoning (AGENT-056),
and tool argument poisoning (AGENT-057). Includes static config analysis
and dynamic inspection support.

New modules: tool_description_analyzer.py (~200 lines, 5-category
poisoning detection), mcp_baseline.py (~530 lines, SHA-256 baseline
drift detection). 14 E2E tests, 3 AVB benchmark samples (WILD-007/008/009).

53 total rules, 1239 tests passing, 10/10 OWASP Agentic Top 10 coverage.

docs: add SEO meta tags and improve PyPI search keywords

- Add og:title, og:description, og:url, keywords meta tags to
  docs/index.html for better search engine indexing
- Update pyproject.toml description with key search terms
- Expand keywords to cover high-intent search queries
- Point documentation URL to GitHub Pages site
docs: link README title to GitHub Pages site
docs: add documentation and PyPI links below README title
docs: add GitHub Pages badge to README

docs: add landing page for GitHub Pages

Static single-page site at docs/index.html with hero, quick start
terminal, threat detection cards, benchmark tables, architecture
pipeline, OWASP coverage grid, and audience section.
fold sections to improve readability

Add Codecov token to CI configuration

Add Codecov token to CI workflow for coverage upload

commit.message: fix codecov and add coverage report
unify Quick Start heading and refine intro messaging

docs: add user-oriented quick start guide to root README

Restructure root README to lead with a one-liner value prop, three
real attack scenarios (Why), and a two-command Quick Start with sample
output. Add plain-language "What It Detects" table and "Who Is This
For" section. Move evaluation, architecture, and technical details
below the fold.

docs: rewrite README with user-oriented quick start guide

Restructure README to lead with "why", real attack scenarios, and a
copy-paste quick start, followed by a plain-language detection summary
and audience section. Technical details moved to the bottom.

chore: bump version to 0.16.0 for PyPI publish

Version was still 0.15.1 which already exists on PyPI.
Update pyproject.toml, version.py, and __init__.py to 0.16.0.

docs: rewrite README for research-grade presentation

- Rewrite README to English-only, research-focused format
  - Evaluation results (94.6% recall, F1=0.91) on first screen
  - Architecture diagram showing 5-scanner pipeline
  - Full OWASP Agentic Top 10 coverage table (40+ rules)
  - Comparison table vs Bandit and Semgrep
  - Limitations section, citation block
- Create README_CN.md for Chinese documentation
- Remove CLAUDE.md from tracking (add to .gitignore)
- Move BENCHMARK-RESULTS to docs/, create COMPETITIVE-COMPARISON.md
- Remove internal dev docs (BENCHMARK-PROGRESS, COMPARISON, improve.md, etc.)
- Update .gitignore for generated reports and internal docs
- Update GitHub repo description and topics

docs: add benchmark results section to README

Add comprehensive benchmark section with AVB evaluation results
(94.6% recall, F1=0.909), 3-tool comparison vs Bandit/Semgrep,
Layer 2 real-world framework scan results (9 targets, 10/10 OWASP),
and unit test summary (1142 passing).

fix: reduce AGENT-034 false positives by 79% via qualified name matching

- python_scanner: require module-qualified names for ambiguous subprocess/eval
  patterns (asyncio.run, re.compile, platform.system no longer falsely trigger)
- python_scanner: add framework internal path suppression for tool_no_input_validation
- python_scanner: multiply boundary confidence into taint finding confidence
- dangerous_operation_analyzer: extend SAFE_TOOL_PATTERNS with 31 new patterns
- semgrep_adapter: enumerate files explicitly to bypass git-tracking limitation
- Benchmark: T9 226→155 (-31%), T6 39→25 (-36%), T7 55→39 (-29%), T8 20→10 (-50%)
- AVB: 94.6% recall, 87.5% precision unchanged (zero TP loss)
- 1142 unit tests passing, 0 regressions
docs: add benchmark infrastructure progress summary
chore: remove PROMPT-v0.14.0-adjustment.txt
fix: add type annotations to fix mypy Attribute/expr assignment errors
fix: remove unnecessary f-string prefix (F541 lint error)

feat: taint tracking, CWE mapping, benchmark harness v2, and scanner enhancements

- Add TaintTracker for intra-function data flow analysis (AGENT-034 precision)
- Add suppression model for baseline/fingerprint-based finding management
- Enhance python_scanner with taint-aware tool input validation
- Enhance mcp_config_scanner with new MCP security rules
- Improve semantic_analyzer with framework-aware credential filtering
- Upgrade SARIF formatter with CWE URI support and severity mapping
- Add tier-to-severity mapping and rule-specific severity overrides
- Expand ASI coverage rules (asi_coverage_v030.yaml)
- Add new MCP security rules (mcp_security_v030.yaml)
- Update benchmark oracle datasets and harness adapter for v2 eval
- Add oracle_eval improvements for partial matching and scoring
- Add GitHub Action (action.yml) enhancements
- Add docs: README, security analysis spec, blog posts, evaluation report
- Add example outputs and test fixtures
- Add test_taint_tracker, test_baseline, test_cwe_mapping, test_formatters

fix: allow -- separator and flags in inspect stdio target

Click was parsing flags like -y as its own options instead of passing
them through to the target argument. Fix by setting
ignore_unknown_options=True and using click.UNPROCESSED type.

Before (broken):
  agent-audit inspect stdio -- npx -y @mcp/server /tmp
  Error: No such option: -y

After (works):
  agent-audit inspect stdio -- npx -y @mcp/server /tmp
  ✓ Connected
docs: add pipx and fallback install instructions to README

docs: pre-release documentation fixes for v0.15.1

- Add LICENSE (MIT) and CONTRIBUTING.md
- Add SECURITY.md with vulnerability reporting policy
- Add docs/ROADMAP.md and docs/COMPARISON.md
- Migrate issue templates from .md to .yml format
- Unify GitHub Action references to HeadyZhang/agent-audit@v1
- Update version tags in CI-INTEGRATION.md and STABILITY.md to v0.15.1

docs(rules): add Rules by Scanner section and fix version

  MCPConfigScanner (9 rules), PrivilegeScanner (3 rules)
- List detection methods for each rule by scanner
- Fix version number from v0.16.0 to v0.15.1 to match current release

docs: rewrite README for open-source release

- Add positioning: "first open-source static analyzer for AI agent code"
- Document 5 detection engines with capabilities
- Add "Under the Hood" section with engine details
- Complete OWASP Agentic Top 10 coverage table (40 rules)
- Add 3 GitHub Action workflow examples (basic, PR comment, scheduled)
- Document confidence tiering (BLOCK/WARN/INFO)
- Link to docs/RULES.md for full rule reference

chore: remove internal dev files, update architecture docs

- Remove 17 internal development files from tracking:
  - PROMPT-*.md (Claude Code prompts)
  - *-EXECUTION-SUMMARY.md (execution logs)
  - *-BENCHMARK-RESULTS.md (benchmark logs)
  - docs/v*.0-*-spec.md (version specs)
  - docs/v*-verification-report.md (internal reports)

- Update .gitignore to prevent future commits of:
  - Claude Code prompts and execution summaries
  - Version-specific specs and reports
  - Benchmark results (generated)
  - Internal execution plans

- Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md to v0.15.1:
  - Refresh directory structure
  - Update module dependency graph
  - Document analysis layer (v0.10+)
  - Update rule coverage table (40 rules, 10/10 ASI)
  - Add confidence-based tiering section
chore: bump version to 0.15.1
Add LICENSE file

commit.message:
docs: add public release documentation and issue templates

## New Documentation
- docs/STABILITY.md: API stability policy with interface stability levels
- docs/RULES.md: Complete rule reference for all 40 rules with fix guidance
- docs/CI-INTEGRATION.md: CI/CD integration guide for 6 platforms

## Issue Templates
- .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md: Bug report template
- .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md: Feature request template
- .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/fp_fn_report.md: False positive/negative report template
- .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml: Template chooser configuration

## Updated Documentation
- CONTRIBUTING.md: Enhanced with detailed test instructions, PR workflow, code style
- README.md: Added "Understanding Results" section, documentation links, simplified rules

This prepares agent-audit for public release with:
- Clear stability guarantees for rule IDs, CLI, and output formats
- Comprehensive rule documentation with CWE/OWASP mappings
- Multi-platform CI/CD examples (GitHub Actions, GitLab, Jenkins, Azure)
- Structured issue templates for community interaction

feat(v0.12.0): Semantic Layer Refinement - Insert Ambiguity Fix & OpenAI Function Calling

## v0.11.0 - Correct Semantic Layer (included)
- AGENT-034: Tool entry point gate (no blacklists)
- AGENT-018: Method whitelist gate (no variable name guessing)
- Core principle: "Identify what to check (finite), not what to exclude (infinite)"

## v0.12.0 - Refinements
### Phase 1: Fix insert Ambiguity
- Split methods into UNAMBIGUOUS (17) and AMBIGUOUS (2: insert, upsert)
- Added file_imports parameter for import-aware detection
- list.insert() no longer triggers FP without llama_index import

### Phase 2: OpenAI Function Calling Detection
- Added FileToolContext for file-level tool analysis
- Functions registered via tools=[func1, func2] now detected as Tool entry points
- Supports both OpenAI and Anthropic function calling patterns

## Test Results
- 1039 tests passing (+91 new v0.12.0 tests)
- All 7 benchmark scenarios passing
- Backward compatibility maintained

## Key Metrics
- SWE-agent AGENT-034: 33 → 0 FP
- Generative Agents AGENT-018: 11 → 0 FP
- OpenAI function + eval: Now detected

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat(v0.9.0): P0 false positive reduction based on benchmark analysis

Key improvements:

1. AGENT-004 Mask Detection
   - Added MASK_PATTERNS to detect redacted values (******, [REDACTED], etc.)
   - Eliminates 520 Gorilla FPs from masked training data

2. AGENT-001 eval/ Path Fix
   - Added IMPLEMENTATION_EXEMPT_PATTERNS for /tasks/impl/, /tools/, /src/
   - Prevents downgrading real implementation code in eval/ directories

3. Same-Line Deduplication
   - Added deduplicate_findings() with rule subsumption
   - AGENT-001 subsumes AGENT-047/034 on same line
   - Reduces redundant findings on subprocess.Popen(shell=True)

4. ML Data File Classification
   - Added DATA_FILE_PATTERNS for train.json, eval_data/, gorilla_openfunctions
   - Classified as FIXTURE context for automatic suppression

5. AGENT-049 Unsafe Deserialization (NEW)
   - Detects pickle.load, torch.load, joblib.load, dill, cloudpickle
   - ASI-04 Supply Chain vulnerability coverage
   - Checks for weights_only=True safe mode in torch.load

6. Expanded Agent Context Detection
   - Added AGENT_CONTEXT_PATTERNS for OpenAI/Anthropic API calls
   - Detects Agent code without @tool decorators (e.g., Generative Agents)

Benchmark results:
- Gorilla: 604 → 67 findings (-89%), AGENT-004: 520 → 0
- MLAgentBench: 75 → 49 findings (-35%)
- Total: 1,262 → 633 findings (-50%)

All 948 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve ruff lint errors (F401, F841, E402)

- context_classifier.py: Remove unused Path, Dict imports
- rule_context_config.py: Remove unused Optional, move import re to top
- semantic_analyzer.py: Remove unused is_localhost_url, get_env_tracer, tracer
- python_scanner.py: Remove unused imports, keep is_localhost_url (used)

feat: agent-audit v0.8.0 - Context-Aware Analysis & Tier Calibration

New Features:
- Context Classifier: Distinguishes test/example/production code
- Environment Tracer: Tracks credential sources (env vars vs hardcoded)
- Rule Context Config: Per-rule context-aware severity adjustment
- Placeholder Detector: Enhanced detection of example/placeholder values

Improvements:
- Semantic Analyzer: Better false positive reduction
- Privilege Scanner: Improved context awareness
- Python Scanner: Enhanced pattern detection
- Terminal Formatter: Better tier-based output display

Documentation:
- v0.8.0 comparison data and verification report
- Execution summaries for benchmark optimization

Tests:
- New test suites for context classifier and tier calibration
- Updated existing tests for new functionality

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

release: agent-audit v0.7.0 - False Positive Reduction

## Benchmark Results
- T5 (deepagents): 142 → 88 findings (38% reduction) ✅
- T9 (crewAI): 713 → 183 findings (74% reduction) ✅
- OWASP Coverage: 10/10 ✅
- All 881 tests pass ✅

## New Features

### 1. Dangerous Operation Analyzer
- New module: analysis/dangerous_operation_analyzer.py
- Only triggers AGENT-034 when parameters flow to dangerous operations
- Recognizes safe tool patterns (get_, fetch_, list_, search_, etc.)

### 2. Framework Internal Path Detection
- New module: analysis/framework_detector.py
- Reduces confidence for findings in framework paths (crewai/, langchain_core/)
- T9 AGENT-004: 286 → 1 (99.6% reduction)

### 3. Test File Confidence Reduction
- Returns low confidence (0.30) for test files
- Prevents false positives from test fixtures and mocks

### 4. Finding Deduplication
- Added _deduplicate_findings() in engine.py
- Removes AGENT-027 when AGENT-010 already fires on same line
- Prevents duplicate ASI-01 findings

## Files Changed
- analysis/dangerous_operation_analyzer.py (new)
- analysis/framework_detector.py (new)
- analysis/semantic_analyzer.py (modified)
- scanners/python_scanner.py (modified)
- rules/engine.py (modified)

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat(v0.6.0): implement Pattern Priority System and Confidence Matrix

Phase 3: Pattern Priority System
- Add PatternPriority enum (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, GENERIC)
- Create SECRET_PATTERNS_V2 with priority-ordered patterns
- Implement priority-based pattern matching in _scan_line_with_priority()
- Higher priority matches block lower priority patterns at same position

Phase 4: Confidence Matrix
- Create confidence_matrix.py module
- Add ConfidenceAdjustment dataclass with multiplier rules
- Implement calculate_final_confidence() with adjustment application
- Add should_suppress() and get_tier_from_confidence() utilities
- Define CONFIDENCE_ADJUSTMENTS for UUID, data identifier, placeholder patterns

Export all new modules from analysis/__init__.py

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat: v0.6.0 - False positive reduction for UUID/data identifier patterns

- Add IdentifierAnalyzer module for variable name semantic analysis
- Add UUID format detection to reduce false positives on data tokens
- Integrate identifier + UUID analysis into SemanticAnalyzer Stage 2
- Add comprehensive tests (69 test cases) for identifier classification

Key improvements:
- UUID patterns (32 hex chars) with data identifier variable names now
  get confidence < 0.30 instead of BLOCK tier
- Real credentials (sk-proj-, ghp_, AKIA) maintain BLOCK tier detection
- Variable names like sample_token, data_id, scene_token are recognized
  as data identifiers and demoted accordingly

Benchmark validation:
- AgentPoison repo: UUID false positives eliminated
- 787 tests pass (no regressions)
- mypy/ruff: clean

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

fix: remove unused imports to pass ruff lint

- semantic_analyzer.py: Remove unused analyze_credential_value, CredentialAnalysis, Assignment
- treesitter_parser.py: Remove unused Sequence import
- privilege_scanner.py: Remove unused Dict, Any imports
- ignore.py: Fix noqa-like comment to avoid ruff warning

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat: release v0.5.2 - Industrial-grade benchmark & OWASP Agentic Top 10 full coverage

## Core Features
- Complete OWASP Agentic Top 10 (ASI-01~10) coverage with 45+ detection rules
- Agent-Vuln-Bench v1.0: 12 KNOWN CVEs + 6 WILD patterns + 2 NOISE projects
- Ground Truth v2.2: 81 samples, 218 vulnerability annotations
- Precision 98.51%, Recall 100%, F1-Score 99.25%

## New Detection Rules (v0.5.x)
- AGENT-043: Daemon privilege escalation
- AGENT-044: Sudoers NOPASSWD configuration
- AGENT-045: Browser automation without sandbox
- AGENT-046: System credential store access
- AGENT-047: Subprocess execution without sandbox
- AGENT-048: Extension permission boundaries

## v0.5.2 Micro-Patch
- AGENT-043 tightened daemon detection
- AGENT-046 credential store deduplication
- AGENT-047 extended safe command list
- Risk Score v2 formula with natural log scaling

## Benchmark Infrastructure
- precision_recall.py: Per-ASI recall metrics
- quality_gates_v2.yaml: Layer 1/2 thresholds
- Agent-Vuln-Bench harness with SWE-bench style evaluation
- Multi-tool comparison (vs Bandit, Semgrep)

## Tests
- 716 tests passing
- Full ASI category coverage validation

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

fix: reduce secret scanner false positives + config discovery bug

Secret scanner improvements:
- Skip class/function definitions, import statements
- Filter env variable lookups (os.environ, getenv, get_from_*)
- Filter secure wrappers (SecretStr, Secret, etc.)
- Detect PascalCase class names that match AWS 40-char pattern
- Validate generic patterns (api_key=, token=) have literal secret values

Config discovery fix:
- Only search scan target and its parents, not CWD
- Handle None value when YAML 'ignore:' has only comments

Tested with langchain: 8 -> 0 false positives

fix: config file discovery and exclude/ignore support

- Search for config in scan target dir, CWD, and parent directories
- Support wildcard rule_id: "*" to match all rules in ignore config
- Parse and apply scan.exclude patterns for file discovery
- Fix path matching to work with relative patterns against absolute paths
- Add backward compatibility for exclude_paths parameter in scanners
- Handle None values in YAML config gracefully

cross-platform + coverage 80%

Cross-platform improvements:
- Add utils/compat.py with IS_WINDOWS, IS_MACOS, normalize_path(), home_config_dir()
- Add Windows event loop fix to __main__.py (WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy)
- Add encoding="utf-8" to all write_text() calls
- Add CREATE_NO_WINDOW flag for Windows subprocess creation
- Add path normalization to Location model for consistent forward slashes
- Add Windows-equivalent sensitive resource patterns

Test coverage improvements (310 tests, 81% coverage):
- Add test_utils/test_compat.py and test_compat_extended.py
- Add test_scanners/test_config_scanner.py
- Add test_scanners/test_mcp_inspector.py
- Add test_scanners/test_mcp_config_scanner_ext.py
- Add test_scanners/test_python_scanner_ext.py
- Add test_cli/test_init_command.py
- Add test_cli/test_inspect_command.py
- Add test_cli/test_inspect_render.py
- Add test_utils/test_mcp_client.py
Phase 1-9 complete: 125 tests, 64% coverage

releases.name: v0.18.2
releases.body:
## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- **Fix rule loading path** — `scan.py` now correctly resolves `agent_audit/rules/builtin/` (3 parents instead of 2), so PyPI installs load all rules properly
- **Sync all 5 YAML rule files** into the package — adds `langchain_security_v030.yaml` and `mcp_security_v030.yaml`, updates `asi_coverage_v030.yaml`
- **Fix unused imports** flagged by ruff (F401)
- **Fix mypy type errors** in skill scanner integration
- **Use dynamic version** in e2e tests instead of hardcoded string

### New Features
- **SKILL.md security scanners** (AGENT-058~064) — detects obfuscated shell commands, daemon persistence, sandbox overrides, and supply chain risks in Claude Code skill files
- **New rules**: AGENT-052 (sensitive data logging), AGENT-053 (agent self-modification)

### Full Changelog
https://github.com/HeadyZhang/agent-audit/compare/v0.17.0...v0.18.2

## Install
\`\`\`bash
pip install agent-audit==0.18.2
\`\`\`

releases.name: v0.16.0 — Competitive Benchmarking & FP Optimization
releases.body:
## Highlights

- **94.6% recall** on Agent-Vuln-Bench (19 samples, 3 vulnerability categories)
- **3.2x recall advantage** over Bandit (29.7%), **3.5x** over Semgrep (27.0%)
- **100% Set B coverage** — only tool that audits MCP configurations
- **79% AGENT-034 false positive reduction** via qualified name matching
- **10/10 OWASP Agentic Top 10** categories covered
- **1142 unit tests** passing, zero regressions

## Changes

### Bug Fixes — AGENT-034 False Positive Reduction

- **Qualified name matching** — `re.compile()`, `asyncio.run()`, `platform.system()` no longer falsely trigger
- **Framework path suppression** — Framework internal code suppresses `tool_no_input_validation`
- **Extended safe tool patterns** — 31 additional safe function patterns
- **Boundary confidence multiplier** — More accurate confidence scoring

### Layer 2 Scan Results

| Target | Findings | Delta |
|--------|----------|-------|
| T6 openai-agents-python | 25 | -36% |
| T7 adk-python | 40 | -27% |
| T8 agentscope | 10 | -50% |
| T9 crewAI | 155 | -31% |

releases.name: Agent Audit v0.15.1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Security scanner for AI agents and MCP configurations based on OWASP Agentic Top 10 (2026)
releases.body:
What It Is                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                
  CLI static analysis tool — "ESLint for AI agents". Detects vulnerabilities in LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, and MCP server      
  configurations before they reach production.                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                
  Key Stats                                                                                                                     
  ┌────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┐                                                                          
  │     Metric     │              Value              │                                                                          
  ├────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┤                                                                          
  │ Rules          │ 40 built-in rules               │                                                                          
  ├────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┤                                                                          
  │ OWASP Coverage │ 10/10 ASI categories            │                                                                          
  ├────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┤                                                                          
  │ Output Formats │ Terminal, JSON, SARIF, Markdown │                                                                          
  ├────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┤                                                                          
  │ Python Support │ 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12           │                                                                          
  └────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┘                                                                          
  What It Detects                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                
  - ASI-01 Goal Hijacking: Prompt injection, missing goal validation                                                            
  - ASI-02 Tool Misuse: Command injection, SQL injection, unsanitized inputs                                                    
  - ASI-03 Privilege Abuse: Excessive permissions, long-lived credentials                                                       
  - ASI-04 Supply Chain: Unverified MCP servers, unpinned dependencies                                                          
  - ASI-05 Code Execution: Unsandboxed eval/exec, data exfiltration chains                                                      
  - ASI-06 Memory Poisoning: Unsanitized memory writes, unbounded history                                                       
  - ASI-07 Inter-Agent Comms: Unencrypted channels                                                                              
  - ASI-08 Cascading Failures: Missing circuit breakers, no error handling                                                      
  - ASI-09 Trust Exploitation: Missing human approval, impersonation                                                            
  - ASI-10 Rogue Agents: No kill switch, self-modification risk                                                                 
                                                                                                                                
  Quick Start                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                
  pip install agent-audit                                                                                                       
  agent-audit scan .                                                                                                            
  agent-audit scan . --format sarif --output results.sarif --fail-on high                                                       
                                                                                                                                
  Links                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                
  - Rule Reference: docs/RULES.md                                                                                               
  - CI Integration: docs/CI-INTEGRATION.md                                                                                      
  - API Stability: docs/STABILITY.md                                                                                            
  - PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/agent-audit/

readme_html:
Agent Audit

Find security vulnerabilities in your AI agent code before they reach production.

Why Agent Security Fails in Production

AI agents are not just chatbots. They execute code, call tools, and touch real systems, so one unsafe input path can become a production incident.

  • Prompt injection rewrites agent intent through user-controlled context
  • Unsafe tool inputs can reach subprocess/eval and become command execution
  • MCP configuration mistakes can leak credentials and expand access unintentionally

If your team ships agent features, owns CI security gates, or operates MCP servers and tool integrations, this is a high-probability risk surface rather than an edge case. You likely need this before every merge if agent code can trigger tools, commands, or external systems.

Agent Audit catches these issues before deployment with an analysis core designed for agent workflows today: tool-boundary taint tracking, MCP configuration auditing, and semantic secret detection, with room to extend into learning-assisted detection over time.

Think of it as security linting for AI agents, with 53 rules mapped to the OWASP Agentic Top 10 (2026).

Quick Start in 6 Lines

  1. Install
pip install agent-audit
  1. Scan your project
agent-audit scan ./your-agent-project
  1. Interpret and gate in CI
# Show only high+ findings
agent-audit scan . --severity high

# Fail CI when high+ findings exist
agent-audit scan . --fail-on high

--severity controls what is reported. --fail-on controls when the command exits with code 1.

Sample report output:

╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Agent Audit Security Report                                                  │
│ Scanned: ./your-agent-project                                                │
│ Files analyzed: 2                                                            │
│ Risk Score: 8.4/10 (HIGH)                                                    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

BLOCK -- Tier 1 (Confidence >= 90%) -- 16 findings

  AGENT-001: Command Injection via Unsanitized Input
    Location: agent.py:21
    Code: result = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True)

  AGENT-010: System Prompt Injection Vector in User Input Path
    Location: agent.py:13
    Code: system_prompt = f"You are a helpful {user_role} assistant..."

  AGENT-041: SQL Injection via String Interpolation
    Location: agent.py:31
    Code: cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '{query}'")

  AGENT-031: Mcp Sensitive Env Exposure
    Location: mcp_config.json:1
    Code: env: {"API_KEY": "sk-a***"}

  ... and 15 more

Summary:
  BLOCK: 16 | WARN: 2 | INFO: 1
  Risk Score: =========================----- 8.4/10 (HIGH)

Validation snapshot (as of 2026-02-19, v0.16 benchmark set): 94.6% recall, 87.5% precision, 0.91 F1, with 10/10 OWASP Agentic Top 10 coverage across 9 open-source targets.
Details: Benchmark Results | Competitive Comparison

What It Detects

Category What goes wrong Example rule
Injection attacks User input flows to exec(), subprocess, SQL AGENT-001, AGENT-041
Prompt injection User input concatenated into system prompts AGENT-010
Leaked secrets API keys hardcoded in source or MCP config AGENT-004, AGENT-031
Missing input validation @tool functions accept raw strings without checks AGENT-034
Unsafe MCP servers No auth, no version pinning, overly broad permissions AGENT-005, AGENT-029, AGENT-030, AGENT-033
MCP tool poisoning Hidden instructions or data exfiltration in tool descriptions AGENT-056, AGENT-057
MCP tool shadowing Multiple servers register identical tool names to override behavior AGENT-055
MCP rug pull / drift Server tools change after initial security audit AGENT-054
No guardrails Agent runs without iteration limits or human approval AGENT-028, AGENT-037
Unrestricted code execution Tools run eval() or shell=True without sandboxing AGENT-035

Full coverage of all 10 OWASP Agentic Security categories. Framework-specific detection for LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, and AgentScope. See all rules ->

OpenClaw Support

Agent Audit is available as an OpenClaw skill on ClawHub:

npx clawhub@latest install agent-audit-scanner

Once installed, ask your OpenClaw agent:

  • "Scan my installed skills for security issues"
  • "Is this new skill safe?"
  • "Audit my OpenClaw config"

The scanner covers all 10 OWASP Agentic AI threat categories and has been validated against 18,899 ClawHub skills at 80% precision.

Who Is This For

  • Agent developers building with LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI Agents SDK, or raw function-calling -- run it before every deploy
  • Security engineers reviewing agent codebases -- get a structured report in SARIF for GitHub Security tab
  • Teams shipping MCP servers -- validate your mcp.json / claude_desktop_config.json for secrets, auth gaps, and supply chain risks

Usage

# Scan a project
agent-audit scan ./my-agent

# JSON output for scripting
agent-audit scan ./my-agent --format json

# SARIF output for GitHub Code Scanning
agent-audit scan . --format sarif --output results.sarif

# Only fail CI on critical findings
agent-audit scan . --fail-on critical

# Inspect a live MCP server (read-only, never calls tools)
agent-audit inspect stdio -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem /tmp

Baseline Scanning

Track only new findings across commits:

# Save current state as baseline
agent-audit scan . --save-baseline baseline.json

# Only report new findings not in baseline
agent-audit scan . --baseline baseline.json --fail-on-new

GitHub Actions

name: Agent Security Scan
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  audit:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: HeadyZhang/agent-audit@v1
        with:
          path: '.'
          fail-on: 'high'
          upload-sarif: 'true'
Input Description Default
path Path to scan .
format Output format: terminal, json, sarif, markdown sarif
severity Minimum severity to report low
fail-on Exit with error at this severity high
baseline Baseline file for incremental scanning -
upload-sarif Upload SARIF to GitHub Security tab true

Evaluation Results

Evaluated on Agent-Vuln-Bench (19 samples across 3 vulnerability categories), compared against Bandit and Semgrep:

Tool Recall Precision F1
agent-audit 94.6% 87.5% 0.91
Bandit 1.8 29.7% 100% 0.46
Semgrep 1.x 27.0% 100% 0.43
Category agent-audit Bandit Semgrep
Set A -- Injection / RCE 100% 68.8% 56.2%
Set B -- MCP Configuration 100% 0% 0%
Set C -- Data / Auth 84.6% 0% 7.7%

Neither Bandit nor Semgrep can parse MCP configuration files -- they achieve 0% recall on agent-specific configuration vulnerabilities (Set B).

Full evaluation details: Benchmark Results | Competitive Comparison

How It Works

Source Files (.py, .json, .yaml, .env, ...)
        |
        +-- PythonScanner ---- AST Analysis ---- Dangerous Patterns
        |        |                                Tool Metadata
        |        +-- TaintTracker --------------- Source->Sink Reachability
        |        +-- DangerousOperationAnalyzer - Tool Boundary Detection
        |
        +-- SecretScanner ---- Regex Candidates
        |        +-- SemanticAnalyzer ----------- 3-Stage Filtering
        |              (Known Formats -> Entropy/Placeholder -> Context)
        |
        +-- MCPConfigScanner -- Server Provenance / Path Permissions / Auth
        |
        +-- PrivilegeScanner -- Daemon / Sudoers / Sandbox / Credential Store
                 |
                 v
            RuleEngine -- 53 Rules x OWASP Agentic Top 10 -- Findings

Key technical contributions:

  • Tool-boundary-aware taint analysis -- Tracks data flow from @tool function parameters to dangerous sinks (eval, subprocess.run, cursor.execute), with sanitization detection. Only triggers when a confirmed tool entry point has unsanitized parameters flowing to dangerous operations.

  • MCP configuration auditing -- Parses claude_desktop_config.json and MCP gateway configs to detect unverified server sources, overly broad filesystem permissions, missing authentication, unpinned package versions, tool description poisoning, cross-server tool shadowing, and baseline drift (rug pull) -- a category entirely missed by existing SAST tools.

  • Three-stage semantic credential detection -- (1) Regex candidate discovery with priority tiers, (2) value analysis with known-format matching, entropy scoring, and placeholder/UUID exclusion, (3) context adjustment by file type, test patterns, and framework schema detection.

Threat Coverage

53 detection rules covering all 10 categories of the OWASP Agentic Top 10 (2026):

OWASP Category Rules Example Detections
ASI-01 Agent Goal Hijack 6 Prompt injection, tool description poisoning, argument poisoning
ASI-02 Tool Misuse 9 @tool input to subprocess without validation
ASI-03 Identity & Privilege 4 Daemon privilege escalation, >10 MCP servers
ASI-04 Supply Chain 7 Unverified MCP source, tool shadowing, baseline drift (rug pull)
ASI-05 Code Execution 3 eval/exec in tool without sandbox
ASI-06 Memory Poisoning 2 Unsanitized input to vector store upsert
ASI-07 Inter-Agent Comm 1 Multi-agent over HTTP without TLS
ASI-08 Cascading Failures 3 AgentExecutor without max_iterations
ASI-09 Trust Exploitation 6 Critical ops without human_in_the_loop
ASI-10 Rogue Agents 3 No kill switch, no behavior monitoring

Real-World Validation

Scanned 9 open-source projects to validate detection quality:

Target Project Findings OWASP Categories
T1 damn-vulnerable-llm-agent 4 ASI-01, ASI-02, ASI-06
T2 DamnVulnerableLLMProject 41 ASI-01, ASI-02, ASI-04
T3 langchain-core 3 ASI-01, ASI-02
T6 openai-agents-python 25 ASI-01, ASI-02
T7 adk-python 40 ASI-02, ASI-04, ASI-10
T8 agentscope 10 ASI-02
T9 crewAI 155 ASI-01, ASI-02, ASI-04, ASI-07, ASI-08, ASI-10
T10 MCP Config (100-tool server) 8 ASI-02, ASI-03, ASI-04, ASI-05, ASI-09
T11 streamlit-agent 6 ASI-01, ASI-04, ASI-08

10/10 OWASP Agentic Top 10 categories detected across targets. Quality gate: PASS.

Comparison with Existing Tools

Capability agent-audit Bandit Semgrep
Agent-specific threat model (OWASP Agentic Top 10) Yes No No
MCP configuration auditing Yes No No
Tool-boundary taint analysis Yes No No
@tool decorator awareness Yes No No
Semantic credential detection Yes Basic Basic
General Python security Partial Yes Yes
Multi-language support Python-focused Python Multi

agent-audit is complementary to general-purpose SAST tools. It targets the security gap specific to AI agent applications that existing tools cannot address.

Configuration

# .agent-audit.yaml
scan:
  exclude: ["tests/**", "venv/**"]
  min_severity: low
  fail_on: high

ignore:
  - rule_id: AGENT-003
    paths: ["auth/**"]
    reason: "Auth module legitimately communicates externally"

allowed_hosts:
  - "api.openai.com"

Current Scope

  • Current core is static analysis: Does not execute code and may miss runtime-only logic vulnerabilities.
  • Intra-procedural taint analysis: Tracks data flow within functions; no cross-function or cross-module tracking yet.
  • Python-focused: Primary support for Python source and MCP JSON configs. Limited pattern matching for other languages.
  • Framework coverage: Deep support for LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, AgentScope. Other frameworks use generic @tool detection rules.
  • False positives: Mitigated through semantic analysis, framework detection, and allowlists; ongoing optimization (79% FP reduction in v0.16).

Documentation

  • Technical Specification -- Detection methodology and analysis pipeline
  • Benchmark Results -- Detailed Agent-Vuln-Bench evaluation
  • Competitive Comparison -- Three-tool analysis vs Bandit and Semgrep
  • Rule Reference -- Complete rule catalog with CWE mappings and remediation
  • Architecture -- Internal design and extension points
  • CI/CD Integration -- GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, Azure DevOps

Development

git clone https://github.com/HeadyZhang/agent-audit
cd agent-audit/packages/audit
poetry install
poetry run pytest ../../tests/ -v  # 1239 tests

See CONTRIBUTING.md for full development setup and PR guidelines.

Citation

If you use agent-audit in your research, please cite:

@software{agent_audit_2026,
  author = {Zhang, Haiyue},
  title = {Agent Audit: Static Security Analysis for AI Agent Applications},
  year = {2026},
  url = {https://github.com/HeadyZhang/agent-audit},
  note = {Based on OWASP Agentic Top 10 (2026) threat model}
}

Acknowledgments

  • OWASP Agentic Top 10 for 2026
  • CWE Top 25

License

MIT -- see LICENSE.
